commit.message:
Fix scroll architecture: slides fill viewport exactly, no white space

Reverted from body-scroll to slide-scroll approach. Each slide is
position: absolute; inset: 0 (fills viewport exactly). The slide
itself is the single scroll container — no embedded scroll-content
areas with their own max-height/overflow.

- Deck: back to height: 100svh; overflow: hidden (no content leak)
- Slides: position absolute, inset 0, overflow-y auto (one scroll)
- Active slide: no position change (stays absolute, no white space)
- scroll-content: still overflow: visible (no nested scrollbars)
- Auto-scroll: back to slideEl.scrollTop (not window.scrollTo)
- Removed goToSlide transition position hack (not needed)
- Kiosk scrollbar hiding on .slide elements
- Short slides cannot scroll (content fits viewport exactly)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: meta-question detection + remove insights placeholder

- Move META-QUESTION section to top of prompt so LLM checks it FIRST
- Explicitly tell LLM to explain previous computation, not recompute

  verbatim as "insight 1 with numbers + action")

- PortfolioInsight: solPct computed from live SOL price, not cached value
- portfolioPnL24h: add !isFinite(pct) guard to prevent NaN/Infinity display
- portfolioPnL24h: guard solChange > -100 to prevent division by zero

commit.message:
fix: Use git rm --cached to reliably stage file deletions

repo.index.remove() silently fails to stage deletions, producing empty
commits. Switching to repo.git.rm('--cached', '--force') which runs the
git binary directly and correctly stages the removal in the index.

commit.message:
Fix: replace express-session with stateless token auth for Vercel

Sessions in memory don't work on Vercel serverless (each request
can hit a different instance). Replaced with HMAC-signed tokens
stored in httpOnly cookies - fully stateless, works on serverless.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: drop legacy trigger_queue_push_notification to prevent double push (#50)

The old queue-based push system (trigger_queue_push_notification →
push_notification_logs → process-push-queue) was never removed when
trigger_send_push_notification_instant was introduced. Both triggered
on every notifications.insert, causing every user to receive 2 push
notifications for all notification types.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix burned accounts not recovering after unban

Accounts stuck as 'burned' forever because evaluateAccountPhases had
no recovery path. Now when isSuspended=false AND removalRate<=60,
burned accounts transition back to 'ready' or 'warming' based on
karma/age. Next sync will pick up the unban and restore the phase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent settings watcher from resetting input during save

Redesign homepage flag carousel: auto-scroll marquee, flags only

Remove country names and title block. Flags auto-scroll infinitely
using CSS keyframe animation (duplicated set for seamless loop).
Hover pauses animation and scales up the hovered flag with teal glow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Iteration 11: complete Word rendering – styles, borders, table layout

OOXMLStylesParser (new): parses word/styles.xml extracting docDefaults
(Arial 10pt) and per-style bold/italic/color/fontFamily/spacingBefore/After.
OOXMLDocumentBodyParser now initialises run state from style defaults instead
of hardcoded Helvetica 12pt; applies style spacing when no inline override.

New OOXML parsing:
- w:pBdr top/bottom — paragraph border color + width (eighths of a point)
- w:tblGrid/w:gridCol — table column widths in twips → WordTableContent.columnWidthsPt
- w:tcMar top/bottom/left/right — cell margins → WordTableCellMargins
- w:tcBorders — cell border color → WordTableCell.borderColorHex
- w:ind w:hanging — negative first-line indent for list hanging indent

New RenderModels fields:
- WordParagraphContent: borderTopHex/Width, borderBottomHex/Width
- WordTableCell: borderColorHex, margins (WordTableCellMargins)
- WordTableContent: columnWidthsPt
- WordTableCellMargins (new struct): top/bottom/left/right

Renderer improvements:
- drawParagraph: draws CT lines for top/bottom paragraph borders
- drawTable: proportional column widths scaled from tblGrid; cell text
  rendered per-paragraph (CTFramesetter, full bold/color/font) instead of
  plain joined text; cell margins applied to text inset; border color from
  tcBorders; per-cell width used for accurate row height measurement
- rowHeight: paragraph-based measurement using buildSingleParagraphAttrStr
- empty paragraph default font changed from Helvetica 12pt to Arial 10pt

Tests: 8 new unit tests for borderTop/Bottom, cell margins, border color,
columnWidthsPt → 112 total (4 skipped), 0 failures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Live sidechain mode (#206)

* feat(innexus): implement live sidechain mode (Milestone 3, Phase 12)

Add real-time continuous analysis from sidechain audio input, enabling
performers to route live audio into Innexus and play MIDI keys that
inherit the live source's timbral character.

New DSP components:
- SpectralCoringEstimator (Layer 2): lightweight residual estimation
  by zeroing harmonic bins and measuring inter-harmonic energy
- LiveAnalysisPipeline: real-time orchestration of existing analysis
  components (PreProcessing, YIN, STFT, PartialTracker, ModelBuilder)
  with zero audio-thread allocations (FR-008)

New plugin features:
- Sidechain auxiliary audio input bus (stereo, downmixed to mono)
- Input Source parameter (Sample/Sidechain) with 20ms crossfade
- Latency Mode parameter (Low Latency 11.6ms / High Precision 40Hz+)
- State version 3 with backward compatibility for v1/v2 presets
- Confidence-gated freeze reuses existing mechanism (FR-010)
- All existing controls (harmonic level, residual level, brightness,
  transient emphasis) work identically in sidechain mode (FR-016)

Success criteria verified:
- SC-001: Analysis latency <=25ms (measured 11.6ms hop + <1ms overhead)
- SC-002: 40Hz detection in high-precision mode (41Hz within 5Hz margin)
- SC-003: Analysis CPU <5% single-core at 44.1kHz
- SC-004: Analysis+synthesis CPU <8% single-core at 44.1kHz
- SC-005: Source crossfade click-free (<-60dB discontinuity)
- SC-006: Freeze within 2 STFT hops of silence
- SC-007: Residual output RMS >=-60dBFS
- SC-008: Pluginval strictness 5 pass

Tests: 118 innexus test cases, 6232 DSP test cases, all passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(innexus): bump version to 0.3.0 and update changelog for M3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ruinae): add arp MIDI output and state versioning (v2)

Add MIDI Out toggle to arpeggiator that forwards arp note events to a
MIDI output bus, enabling chaining to other instruments. Introduce formal
state version gating (kCurrentStateVersion = 2) so version-1 presets
load without the midiOut field, and future format additions follow the
established if (version >= N) pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(innexus): make sidechain bus inactive by default for AU compatibility

The AU v2 wrapper cannot initialize an instrument (aumu) with active
input buses when the AU plist declares 0 inputs, causing auval to fail
with error -10875 (kAudioUnitErr_FormatNotSupported). Auxiliary sidechain
buses must be inactive by default — the host activates them on demand.

Also documents Innexus plugin in CLAUDE.md and README.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(innexus): add sidechain channel config to AU plist and AUv3 config

The VST3 AU wrapper creates AU IO elements for ALL audio input buses,
including inactive auxiliary ones. Apple's AUBase::Initialize() then
validates the channel count against the plist — if only "0 in / 2 out"
is declared but the wrapper created 1 input element (from the sidechain
bus), it returns -10875 (kAudioUnitErr_FormatNotSupported).

Add "2 in / 2 out" channel configuration to both au-info.plist (AU v2)
and audiounitconfig.h (AUv3) to support the sidechain input bus.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add persistent Playwright browser for CasePeer session management

Keep a headless Chromium browser open permanently to prevent session expiry.
Browser-based keepalive pings replace HTTP-only pings, cookies are synced
to requests.Session automatically, and session recovery is instant instead
of requiring a full re-login (~45s OTP dance).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

security: Restrict model loading to SafeTensors format only

PyTorch pickle files (.bin, .pt) can execute arbitrary code during
deserialization — a known supply chain attack vector. MLX Code now:

- Rejects any model directory that lacks .safetensors files
- Skips non-SafeTensors models during discovery (won't appear in list)
- Throws MLXServiceError.unsafeModelFormat with an explanatory message
  if a user somehow tries to load a rejected model
- Logs a warning when a model is skipped during discovery

All mlx-community models on HuggingFace use SafeTensors by default,
so this has no impact on normal usage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Optimize stream startup latency (~250ms our side)

- Fire P2P before spawning ffmpeg (parallel, not sequential)
- Add -fflags nobuffer, -analyzeduration 0, -probesize 32 for fastest first frame
- Add -flush_packets 1 to reduce output buffering
- Defer return audio ffmpeg to talkback start (off critical video path)
- Fire-and-forget talkback request (don't block video handler)
- Add latency instrumentation logging throughout stream pipeline

commit.message:
fix: map search button not opening on click

The search bar's native stopPropagation on 'click' events prevented
React 18's event delegation (attached at #root) from ever receiving
the click. Replaced with Leaflet's L.DomEvent.disableClickPropagation
which stops at mousedown level, blocking Leaflet map interaction while
still allowing React onClick handlers to fire.

commit.message:
fix(api): use internal ArgoCD URL and add NetworkPolicies (#1456)

## Summary
- Console Platform Status showed "Unknown" for all components due to
ArgoCD unreachable from API pod
- Root cause: `default-deny-all` NetworkPolicies in both `stoa-system`
and `argocd` namespaces blocked cross-namespace traffic, and
`allow-external-egress` excluded private IPs (`10.0.0.0/8`)
- Switch `ARGOCD_URL` from external `https://argocd.gostoa.dev` to
internal `http://argocd-server.argocd.svc.cluster.local` (same hairpin
NAT avoidance pattern as Keycloak)
- Add bidirectional NetworkPolicies: ingress in `argocd` namespace +
egress in `stoa-system` namespace

## Test plan
- [x] Live-tested: NetworkPolicies applied, ConfigMap updated, pods
restarted
- [x] TCP connectivity verified from API pod to argocd-server:8080
- [x] ArgoCD `/api/version` returns 200 (v3.3.0)
- [ ] CI green

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v1.3.0: CSS safety, lazy fade-in, post-save update notice

- class-aio-lazy-load.php: Inject <style id="aio-lazy-css"> in wp_head with
  a @keyframes aio-lazy-in fade-in animation applied to .aio-lazy-loaded and
  .aio-lazy-bg-loaded. Wrapped in prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference so
  users who prefer no animation get an instant swap. Uses only plugin-owned
  CSS classes — zero impact on theme or third-party styles.
- class-aio-admin.php: Clear the GitHub release transient when settings are
  saved (settings-updated=true) so the next page load performs a fresh update
  check instead of serving a potentially 12-hour-old cached result.
- admin/views/settings.php: Show a WP-style warning notice immediately after
  saving settings if a newer plugin version is available, with a direct
  "Update Now" button.
